pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: QNRX surged +51.8% at open (11.9 vs 7.84 prior close) on 2025-04-02 but collapsed -37.6% by EOD (7.42). An 8-K Item 8.01 was filed same day claiming a 'material agreement,' but the full text is unavailable (stub mode). The bear thesis rests on structural absence: (1) No news corroboration in 7-day window despite claimed material catalyst; (2) Fundamental data entirely absent (no revenue, earnings, cash flow available); (3) Company is deeply unprofitable (PE=-0.0011, ROE=-2721%, ROA=-1007%); (4) Recent earnings (2025-03-13) massively missed estimates (-12.24 actual vs -0.5 estimate); (5) No alternative-data footprint (GitHub, Wikipedia, sector-specific adapters all failed/unavailable); (6) Analyst sentiment unchanged (2 Buy, 0 Sell) but descriptor-only and not corroborating the catalyst. The surge appears momentum-driven rather than value-creation. However, evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL because the actual 8-K text is unavailable (stub mode), the company's sector is not explicitly stated, and the specific claim in the 'material agreement' cannot be verified. The structural-absence pattern (no news, no fundamentals, no alt-data, no filing text) is itself bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 asymmetric absence rule, but the absence of ground-truth filing content prevents definitive validation of the catalyst claim. Confidence is LOW due to missing ground-truth filing content and fundamental data. The Surge-Short Agent recommended WATCH pending retrieval of ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data. Risk Agent found zero hard-veto conditions tripped. Given the high uncertainty, missing critical data (8-K text, fundamentals), and the Surge-Short Agent's own WATCH recommendation, the preponderance of evidence does not support entry at this time. The bear thesis is plausible but not sufficiently coherent without the actual catalyst claim verification.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K Item 8.01 'material agreement' is retrieved and describes a genuine, value-accretive transaction (e.g., major contract, partnership, acquisition) with verifiable counterparty and terms, the short thesis weakens significantly.
If fundamental data becomes available showing positive revenue growth, improving margins, or reduced cash burn, the short thesis is invalidated.

If alternative-data adapters (GitHub, sector-specific) return evidence of real operational progress (e.g., developer activity, clinical trial enrollment, regulatory approvals), the short thesis is challenged.
If the stock stabilizes above 9.00 and news coverage emerges supporting the catalyst, momentum may sustain and short thesis becomes riskier.
If macro regime shifts to risk-on and equity_discipline relaxes, valuation skepticism may ease.
risk_flags: Stub-mode 8-K text: cannot verify the actual claim driving the surge; decision is partially blind.
Fundamental data gap: no revenue, earnings, or cash-flow data available; cannot assess true financial health.
Extreme unprofitability: ROE=-2721%, ROA=-1007% suggest either a turnaround story or a value trap; without fundamentals, cannot distinguish.
Recent earnings miss: 2025-03-13 EPS -12.24 vs -0.5 estimate is a massive negative surprise; stock may be repricing downward.
No news corroboration: 7-day news window returned 0 items despite claimed material catalyst; unusual for a listed company.
Analyst ratings unchanged: 2 Buy, 0 Sell as of 2025-04-01; no analyst reaction to the catalyst suggests skepticism or lag.
Macro headwind: Overheat regime with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline argues against speculative rallies.
High intraday volatility: +51.8% open gap followed by -37.6% collapse by EOD suggests panic/momentum reversal.
Multiple stock splits in history (1:12 in 2023, 2:25 in 2022, 1:4 in 2021): suggests distressed capital structure and potential dilution.
Sector unknown: packet does not specify industry; cannot apply sector-specific evidence reasoning.
suggested_improvements: Retrieve full 8-K Item 8.01 text (not stub mode) to verify the actual 'material agreement' claim and assess its credibility.
Obtain fundamental data (10-Q or latest 10-K) to assess revenue, profitability, cash position, and debt levels.
Identify company sector/industry from SEC filings (10-K Item 1) and apply sector-specific evidence reasoning.
Search for press releases or investor relations announcements on the company's website or newswire services to corroborate the catalyst.
Check SEC Form 4 filings for insider trading activity around the surge date; absence of insider buying is bear-thesis evidence.
Retrieve analyst reports or equity research notes to assess whether sell-side analysts have commented on the catalyst.
Monitor next earnings call (if scheduled) for management commentary on the 'material agreement' and forward guidance.
Assess short-interest and borrow availability to evaluate short-squeeze risk.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet v1 lacks several critical features: (1) sec_8k_fulltext is stub mode, preventing verification of the catalyst claim; (2) fundamental_snapshot is entirely null, preventing financial assessment; (3) alternative_data_features are not_connected, preventing corroboration via sector-specific adapters; (4) sector/industry is not explicitly provided, preventing sector-aware reasoning per Pass 6; (5) narrative_price_gap_assessment is not_evaluated (pending Agent 03), so the agent cannot reference upstream narrative analysis. The packet is sufficient for a 'no_trade' or 'watch' decision but insufficient for a high-confidence 'short' recommendation.
recommended_followups: Priority 1: Retrieve full 8-K Item 8.01 text to verify the 'material agreement' claim and assess its materiality and credibility.
Priority 2: Obtain latest 10-Q or 10-K to assess revenue, profitability, cash position, and debt; determine if company is a turnaround or value trap.
Priority 3: Identify sector/industry from SEC filings and apply sector-specific evidence reasoning.
Priority 4: Search for press releases, investor relations announcements, or analyst reports to corroborate the catalyst narrative.
Priority 5: Monitor Form 4 filings for insider trading activity; absence of insider buying around the surge is bear-thesis evidence.
Priority 6: Track next earnings call for management commentary on the 'material agreement' and forward guidance.
Priority 7: Re-evaluate if stock stabilizes above 9.00 and news coverage emerges; if so, momentum may sustain and short thesis becomes riskier.

narrative_event.decision_or_assessment: Catalyst classification and narrative specificity assessment
evidence_missing: company business description or sector classification
narrative_event.reasoning_summary: QNRX filed an 8-K on 2025-04-02 (Item 8.01 Other Events) on the same day as the decision_timestamp. The filing_confirmation block confirms SEC_8K_confirmation=true and filing_date=2025-04-02. However, the full text of the 8-K is returned in stub mode by the sec_8k_fulltext adapter (source_flag=mock_fallback, extraction_status=stub), containing only placeholder text: 'the Company entered into a material agreement described in the attached exhibit.' This is NOT ground-truth filing content and cannot be used to extract the actual catalyst. The news_event_summary block shows status=data_unavailable with 0 items returned and 20 items dropped post-cutoff, meaning no news corroboration is available within the decision window. Polygon News returned 0 rows for the 7-day lookback. The price action is severe: -24.59% over 5 days, -28.10% over 20 days, -68.45% over 60 days, with volume 0.87x the 20-day average. The most recent earnings (2025-03-13, 20 trading days before decision) showed EPS actual=-12.24 vs estimated=-0.5, a massive miss. The company has a history of large negative earnings surprises (2024-11-07: -16.43 vs -15.38; 2024-08-08: -13.64 vs -26.22 beat; 2024-05-09: -38.81 vs -0.76). Analyst sentiment from FMP shows 2 Buy + 1 Strong Buy as of 2025-04-01, unchanged from prior months, suggesting no recent analyst pivot. The macro regime is Overheat/Expansionary with strict_quality_and_valuation_caution discipline. CRITICAL ABSENCE: The 8-K text is stub data. Without the actual filing content, the specific catalyst cannot be identified. The structural absence of news corroboration combined with the stub filing text means the narrative cannot be classified as concrete or vague—it cannot be classified at all. The price decline appears driven by the recent earnings miss (2025-03-13) rather than the 2025-04-02 8-K, but the 8-K itself is the only potential new catalyst on the decision date, and its content is inaccessible.
invalidation_conditions: If the actual 8-K text (when retrieved) reveals a non-material or routine disclosure, the catalyst would be reclassified as vague or non-event
If the price decline is entirely attributable to the 2025-03-13 earnings miss and the 8-K is unrelated, the 2025-04-02 filing is not the true catalyst
If the 8-K text reveals a positive development (partnership, financing, product launch), the narrative would flip from bear to bull
If subsequent news (post-cutoff) reveals the 8-K was a material adverse event, the assessment would need revision

narrative_event.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet architecture does not provide a fallback mechanism when the primary filing-text adapter returns stub data. The sec_8k_fulltext adapter is marked mock_fallback with extraction_status=stub, but there is no secondary adapter or manual-fetch instruction to retrieve the actual filing. This creates a hard blocker for narrative classification when the only potential catalyst is an 8-K whose content is inaccessible. The news_event_summary block correctly filters post-cutoff items, but the absence of any pre-cutoff news items (0 returned) combined with the stub filing text leaves the agent unable to extract a concrete catalyst. The fundamental_snapshot insufficient_evidence status prevents sector-aware reasoning per RULES.md §6 Pass 6, which would otherwise help contextualize the 8-K disclosure.
recommended_followups: Retrieve the actual 8-K filing text from https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1671502/000110465925031240/tm2511344d1_8k.htm using a non-stub parser
Extract Item 8.01 text and any attached exhibits to identify the 'material agreement'
Search for press releases or news articles published on or before 2025-04-02 that reference the 8-K or the agreement
Determine the company's sector and business model from the 10-K or company website
Assess whether the 8-K is the true catalyst for the price decline or if the decline is driven by the 2025-03-13 earnings miss
If the 8-K is a material adverse event (e.g., covenant breach, customer loss, financing failure), classify as credible_catalyst with bear thesis
If the 8-K is a positive development (e.g., partnership, contract win), classify as credible_catalyst with bull thesis
If the 8-K is routine or non-material, classify as vague or non-event

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Narrative-Price Dislocation Verification for QNRX surge candidate
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: QNRX exhibits a severe price collapse (-68.45% over 60d, -28.10% over 20d, -24.59% over 5d) with recent earnings miss (2025-03-13: EPS actual -12.24 vs estimate -0.5, a -1144% miss). An 8-K was filed on 2025-04-02 (Item 8.01, Other Events) at the decision timestamp, but the full text is stub-only and cannot be verified. No news items exist within the cutoff window (polygon_news returned 0 rows). SEC Form 4 shows no insider activity in the last 90 days. Analyst sentiment remains stable (2 Strong Buy + 1 Buy as of 2025-04-01) despite the collapse, suggesting analyst ratings lag price reality. The macro regime is 'Overheat/Expansionary' with strict equity discipline, which argues against long positions in distressed names. Alternative-data adapters (GitHub, Wikipedia) returned zero rows, which is informative absence for a company that should have some public footprint if legitimate. The structural absence of corroborating evidence (no news, no insider buying, no GitHub activity, no Wikipedia article) relative to the public narrative of a company filing 8-Ks and reporting earnings is consistent with a bear thesis: the company is in distress, the recent 8-K may signal further deterioration, and the analyst ratings are stale. The lack of hard-to-fake evidence (insider buying, regulatory approval, material contract disclosure in full text) combined with the earnings miss and price collapse suggests the narrative (if any positive catalyst was implied by the 8-K) is not supported by observable alternative data.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K Item 8.01 text (currently stub) reveals a material positive catalyst (strategic partnership, regulatory approval, financing), the bear thesis weakens.
If insider Form 4 activity appears post-cutoff showing CEO/CFO buying, the distress narrative is contradicted.
If news coverage emerges post-cutoff explaining the 8-K as a positive event, the absence of pre-cutoff news becomes less informative.
If the company announces a reverse merger, acquisition, or bankruptcy, the current price may be justified and the surge candidate classification becomes moot.
risk_flags: Earnings miss of -1144% (actual -12.24 vs estimate -0.5) indicates severe guidance failure or accounting surprise.
Stock has declined 68.45% in 60 days; this is a distressed equity, not a surge candidate.
8-K filed on decision date (Item 8.01) with stub-only text; cannot verify content.
Zero news items in 7-day window; information vacuum during price collapse.
Analyst ratings (2 Strong Buy, 1 Buy) are stale and do not reflect recent earnings miss.
No insider buying despite 90-day window; insiders are not supporting the stock.
Alternative-data absence (GitHub, Wikipedia) suggests limited public presence or legitimacy concerns.
Macro regime is 'Overheat' with strict equity discipline; risk-off environment.
Multiple stock splits (1:12 in 2023, 2:25 in 2022, 1:4 in 2021) suggest chronic dilution and distress.
suggested_improvements: Retrieve and parse the full text of the 2025-04-02 8-K Item 8.01 to determine if it contains a material catalyst or further bad news.
Cross-check the earnings miss against historical guidance and analyst consensus to assess whether this is a one-time miss or a pattern.
Investigate the company's business model and sector to determine what alternative-data sources should be available (e.g., if biotech, check clinical trial registries; if SaaS, check GitHub).
Monitor post-cutoff news and SEC filings for bankruptcy, restructuring, or acquisition announcements.
If this is a surge_short candidate, the structural absence of corroborating evidence is itself bear-thesis input; do not require positive contradiction signals.

If this is a surge_short candidate, the structural absence of positive corroboration (no insider buying, no news, no GitHub, no Wikipedia) is bear-thesis evidence and should be weighted accordingly.
alt_data_verify.narrative_price_gap_rationale: QNRX has declined 68.45% in 60 days and 24.59% in 5 days, with a recent earnings miss of -1144% (actual -12.24 vs estimate -0.5). An 8-K was filed on the decision date, but its content is unknown (stub mode). The structural absence of corroborating alternative data (zero news, zero insider buying, zero GitHub activity, no Wikipedia article, no institutional ownership data) contradicts any positive narrative that might be implied by the 8-K filing. The analyst ratings (2 Strong Buy, 1 Buy) are stale and do not reflect the recent earnings miss. The macro regime is 'Overheat' with strict equity discipline, which argues against long positions. The price gap is high because the stock has collapsed despite analyst support, suggesting the market is pricing in information (distress, deterioration) that is not yet reflected in the stale analyst ratings.

surge_short.reasoning_summary: QNRX surged +51.8% at open (11.9 vs 7.84 prior close) on 2025-04-02, but collapsed -37.6% by EOD (7.42). An 8-K Item 8.01 was filed same day claiming a 'material agreement,' but the full text is unavailable (stub mode). The bear thesis rests on structural absence: (1) No news corroboration in 7-day window despite a claimed material catalyst; (2) Fundamental data entirely absent (no revenue, earnings, cash flow available); (3) Company is deeply unprofitable (PE=-0.0011, ROE=-2721%, ROA=-1007%); (4) Recent earnings (2025-03-13) massively missed estimates (-12.24 actual vs -0.5 estimate); (5) No alternative-data footprint (GitHub, Wikipedia, sector-specific adapters all failed/unavailable); (6) Analyst sentiment unchanged (2 Buy, 0 Sell) but descriptor-only and not corroborating the catalyst. The surge appears to be momentum/narrative-driven rather than value-creation. The absence of news coverage, fundamental data, and alt-data corroboration for a claimed 'material agreement' is itself bear-thesis evidence under the structural-absence-as-signal principle. However, evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL because the actual 8-K text is unavailable (stub mode), the company's sector is not explicitly stated, and the specific claim in the 'material agreement' cannot be verified. Confidence is LOW due to missing ground-truth filing content and fundamental data.
invalidation_conditions: If the 8-K Item 8.01 'material agreement' is retrieved and describes a genuine, value-accretive transaction (e.g., major contract, partnership, acquisition) with verifiable counterparty and terms, the short thesis weakens significantly.
If fundamental data becomes available showing positive revenue growth, improving margins, or reduced cash burn, the short thesis is invalidated.

If alternative-data adapters (GitHub, sector-specific) return evidence of real operational progress (e.g., developer activity, clinical trial enrollment, regulatory approvals), the short thesis is challenged.
If the stock stabilizes above 9.00 and news coverage emerges supporting the catalyst, momentum may sustain and short thesis becomes riskier.
If macro regime shifts to risk-on and equity_discipline relaxes, valuation skepticism may ease.

Multiple stock splits in history (1:12 in 2023, 2:25 in 2022, 1:4 in 2021): suggests distressed capital structure and potential dilution.
Sector unknown: packet does not specify industry; cannot apply sector-specific evidence reasoning.
suggested_improvements: Retrieve full 8-K Item 8.01 text (not stub mode) to verify the actual 'material agreement' claim and assess its credibility.
Obtain fundamental data (10-Q or latest 10-K) to assess revenue, profitability, cash position, and debt levels.
Identify company sector/industry from SEC filings (10-K Item 1) and apply sector-specific evidence reasoning (e.g., FDA for biotech, GitHub for software, capex for industrial).
Search for press releases or investor relations announcements on the company's website or newswire services (not just FMP/Polygon) to corroborate the catalyst.
Check SEC Form 4 filings for insider trading activity around the surge date; absence of insider buying is bear-thesis evidence.
Retrieve analyst reports or equity research notes to assess whether sell-side analysts have commented on the catalyst.
Monitor next earnings call (if scheduled) for management commentary on the 'material agreement' and forward guidance.
Assess short-interest and borrow availability to evaluate short-squeeze risk (note: borrow cost trajectory is not available per architectural constraints).

surge_short.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet is v1 and lacks several critical features: (1) sec_8k_fulltext is stub mode, preventing verification of the catalyst claim; (2) fundamental_snapshot is entirely null, preventing financial assessment; (3) alternative_data_features are not_connected, preventing corroboration via sector-specific adapters; (4) sector/industry is not explicitly provided, preventing sector-aware reasoning per Pass 6; (5) narrative_price_gap_assessment is not_evaluated (pending Agent 03), so the agent cannot reference upstream narrative analysis. The packet is sufficient for a 'no_trade' or 'watch' decision but insufficient for a high-confidence 'short' recommendation. The structural-absence pattern (no news, no fundamentals, no alt-data, no filing text) is itself bear-thesis evidence, but the absence of ground-truth filing content prevents definitive validation of the catalyst claim.
recommended_followups: Priority 1: Retrieve full 8-K Item 8.01 text to verify the 'material agreement' claim and assess its materiality and credibility.
Priority 2: Obtain latest 10-Q or 10-K to assess revenue, profitability, cash position, and debt; determine if company is a turnaround or value trap.
Priority 3: Identify sector/industry from SEC filings and apply sector-specific evidence reasoning (e.g., FDA for biotech, GitHub for software).
Priority 4: Search for press releases, investor relations announcements, or analyst reports to corroborate the catalyst narrative.
Priority 5: Monitor Form 4 filings for insider trading activity; absence of insider buying around the surge is bear-thesis evidence.
Priority 6: Track next earnings call for management commentary on the 'material agreement' and forward guidance.
Priority 7: Assess short-interest, borrow availability, and short-squeeze risk (note: borrow cost trajectory unavailable per architectural constraints).
Priority 8: Re-evaluate if stock stabilizes above 9.00 and news coverage emerges; if so, momentum may sustain and short thesis becomes riskier.

pm.reasoning_summary: QNRX surged +51.8% at open (11.9 vs 7.84 prior close) on 2025-04-02 but collapsed -37.6% by EOD (7.42). An 8-K Item 8.01 was filed same day claiming a 'material agreement,' but the full text is unavailable (stub mode). The bear thesis rests on structural absence: (1) No news corroboration in 7-day window despite claimed material catalyst; (2) Fundamental data entirely absent (no revenue, earnings, cash flow available); (3) Company is deeply unprofitable (PE=-0.0011, ROE=-2721%, ROA=-1007%); (4) Recent earnings (2025-03-13) massively missed estimates (-12.24 actual vs -0.5 estimate); (5) No alternative-data footprint (GitHub, Wikipedia, sector-specific adapters all failed/unavailable); (6) Analyst sentiment unchanged (2 Buy, 0 Sell) but descriptor-only and not corroborating the catalyst. The surge appears momentum-driven rather than value-creation. However, evidence sufficiency is PARTIAL because the actual 8-K text is unavailable (stub mode), the company's sector is not explicitly stated, and the specific claim in the 'material agreement' cannot be verified. The structural-absence pattern (no news, no fundamentals, no alt-data, no filing text) is itself bear-thesis evidence per §5.17 asymmetric absence rule, but the absence of ground-truth filing content prevents definitive validation of the catalyst claim. Confidence is LOW due to missing ground-truth filing content and fundamental data. The Surge-Short Agent recommended WATCH pending retrieval of ground-truth 8-K text and fundamental data. Risk Agent found zero hard-veto conditions tripped. Given the high uncertainty, missing critical data (8-K text, fundamentals), and the Surge-Short Agent's own WATCH recommendation, the preponderance of evidence does not support entry at this time. The bear thesis is plausible but not sufficiently coherent without the actual catalyst claim verification.
